instructions: Return JSONL only: exactly one JSON object per input record and no markdown.
Each JSON object must have keys: record_id, provider, source_type, claim_label, premise_label, relation_label, confidence.
confidence must be a number between 0 and 1.
Do not add explanations, comments, or code fences.

records.text: Hon Member: Kia ora koutou katoa.
E te Mana Whakawā, tēnā koe.
I rise today to speak on the third reading of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2024–2025) Bill.
The Minister of Finance has outlined the Government's commitment to fiscal responsibility while investing in health, education, and infrastructure.
Speaker: The question is that the amendment be agreed to.
Hon Member: I want to acknowledge the importance of the Treaty of Waitangi settlements and the work of the Waitangi Tribunal.
As section 8 of the Treaty of Waitangi Act 1975 makes clear, the Tribunal has the authority to make recommendations on claims.
The principles of partnership, protection, and participation are fundamental to our constitutional arrangements.
(1) This Act is the Legislation (Repeals and Amendments) Act 2024.

(2) In this Act, unless the context otherwise requires,— (a) commencement means the date on which this Act receives the Royal assent; and (b) enactment means an enactment (within the meaning of section 29 of the Legislation Act 2019) that is repealed or amended by Schedule 1.
(3) Section 5 of the Māori Language Act 2016 provides that Te Reo Māori is an official language of New Zealand.
(4) The Minister must, no later than 1 July 2025, prepare a report on the operation of tikanga Māori within the scope of this enactment.
(5) Despite subsection (4), the provisions of this Act do not apply to any taonga found on land to which section 186 of the Resource Management Act 1991 applies.
